name: Cantonese Lemon Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the mushrooms in water for 30 minutes or until soft. Drain and shred finely. Discard tough stems.
2. Remove the skin from one lemon and shred into matchstick-sized strips. Finely grate the skin of the remaining lemon. Squeeze juice from both lemons.
3. In a bowl, combine shredded mushrooms, bamboo shoots, shredded lemon skins, grated lemon skins, lemon juice, lemon extract, sugar, light soy sauce, chicken stock or water, and 1 tbsp dark soy sauce.
4. Split the chicken in half, dry with paper towels, and rub with remaining dark soy sauce. Let stand for 10 minutes.
5. Heat oil to 375°F (190°C) in a skillet. Deep-fry chicken for 5 minutes on each side. Cut into 1 1/2- by 1-inch pieces and place in serving dish.
6. In the same skillet, heat remaining peanut oil over high heat until a drop of water sizzles into steam. Add salt and gingerroot. Stir for 30 seconds or until gingerroot browns slightly. Lower heat to medium and add lemon sauce mixture. Stir for 2 minutes.
7. Add chicken fat and stir for 1 minute. Add cornstarch mixture (cornstarch mixed with water) to thicken gravy, stirring constantly.
8. Pour sauce over chicken in serving dish.
